leagueBriefing: Pumas continues to solidify its status as a title contender, while Necaxa's struggles deepen following a heartbreaking last-minute collapse at home.

games.headline: Memote Martínez delivers late heroics as Pumas cements title credentials in Aguascalientes
games.brief: Pumas dominated possession from the opening whistle but struggled to find the clinical touch required to break down a resilient Necaxa defense. The match seemed destined for a frustrating draw after a disallowed Necaxa goal and several heroic saves from Ezequiel Unsain, but Guillermo 'Memote' Martínez proved to be the difference-maker. Coming off the bench, Martínez buried a 84th-minute header to secure three vital points, moving the university side into the upper echelon of the Clausura standings while leaving the Rayos to rue a nightmare evening that included an early injury to their captain.
bullets: Guillermo 'Memote' Martínez scored the game-winner in the 84th minute shortly after entering the match as a substitute.
Necaxa captain Alexis Peña was forced off in tears due to an injury only 16 minutes into the first half.
Keylor Navas maintained a clean sheet for Pumas, further fueling rumors of an impending contract renewal.
Necaxa had a potential opening goal by Tomás Badaloni overturned by VAR due to a handball early in the second half.
Goalkeeper Ezequiel Unsain kept Necaxa in the fight with several high-profile saves, including a long-range blast from Jordan Carrillo.
games.context: The victory moves Pumas into the top tier of the table as title favorites while Necaxa remains mired in a difficult slump.

games.headline: Rubio brace leads Mazatlán's four-goal explosion in dominant rout of struggling León
games.brief: Mazatlán FC bounced back from a recent slump with an emphatic offensive performance, dismantling a vulnerable León side at El Encanto Stadium. After surviving an early scare, the Cannoners took control through Édgar Yoel Bárcenas before Brian Rubio took over the spotlight with a clinical brace. Despite a brief response from León before halftime and a late consolation goal from Diber Cambindo, Mazatlán never looked in danger, sealing the result with a Josué Ovalle header. The win marks a significant turning point for Sergio Bueno's squad, while 'La Fiera' leaves Sinaloa searching for answers after their fifth loss of the semester.
bullets: Brian Rubio was the man of the match, netting goals in the 38th and 47th minutes to lead the Cannoners' attack.
Édgar Yoel Bárcenas opened the scoring with a stunning left-footed strike in the 24th minute.
León's Diber Cambindo had a mixed night, missing a clear one-on-one in the first minute but scoring a long-range goal in the 75th.
Josué Ovalle effectively ended any hope of a comeback with a powerful header in the 69th minute to make it 4-1.
The victory moves Mazatlán to 10 points in the standings, while León continues to slide down the table.
games.context: Mazatlán revitalizes its bid for a playoff spot while León falls into a deep mid-season slump with its fifth defeat.
